title: Gently Baked Sardinian Chickpea Frittata
description: A delicious Italian dish featuring chickpeas, perfect for a brunch mood, made without onion and garlic, and baked in a gentle way to bring out the umami flavor, served on a traditional talavera plate.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, salt, and black pepper until well combined.
Add the grated parmesan cheese to the bowl and stir until the cheese is fully incorporated into the eggs.
Add the chickpeas, cherry tomatoes, and chopped parsley to the bowl and stir gently until they are evenly distributed throughout the egg mixture.
Brush the talavera plate with olive oil to prevent the frittata from sticking.
Pour the egg mixture into the prepared talavera plate and smooth the top.
Cut a piece of papillote paper to fit over the plate and place it on top of the frittata.
Bake the frittata in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the edges are set and the center is still slightly jiggly.
Remove the frittata from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
